customer_anxiety_profile: John is a veteran, cancer patient on a fixed or constrained income who already owns a solar system (grid-tied, making payments, company unknown). His primary anxiety is cost — he said explicitly 'if it involves a lot of money, I don't have no time.' His schedule is medically uncertain. He is cooperative but passive, and his commitment to the appointment is soft.
unresolved_concerns: Cost anxiety: John said 'if it involves a lot of money, I don't have no time' — rep reassured but did not resolve. Closer must address cost framing early and anchor to value, not price.

System compatibility: John's existing solar system company is unknown ('I can't remember right now'). Closer needs to identify the existing system before proposing a Powerwall to confirm compatibility.
Schedule volatility: John has upcoming doctor's appointments related to cancer treatment. Wednesday 1:30 PM was confirmed as clear, but John's availability is medically contingent.

Orphaned-flow gap: Rep did not confirm whether John's existing system is currently working (beyond the ice/snow issue). The system-working question is a hard-DQ gate per the schema — closer should verify.
sensitive_topics_raised: Cancer diagnosis — John disclosed mid-call while checking his schedule
Veteran status — John mentioned this immediately after the cancer disclosure
Financial constraint — John linked money directly to his willingness to proceed
Existing solar system covered in ice/snow and not producing — John raised this as a frustration at the end of the call
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will call John at 1:30 PM Wednesday at the phone number used on this call
The specialist will go through battery options and 'what the numbers look like'
closer_should_know: John's ice-covered panels are the strongest value anchor for the Powerwall pitch — he volunteered this pain point unprompted at 15:17. Lead with that. Do not open with price. John's cost anxiety is unresolved and his health situation makes him a flight risk if the opener feels like a sales call rather than a solution to his specific problem.
